prompt: Top-down flat-lay on a sawhorse-supported plywood workbench, scattered: a worn yellow plastic construction calculator with chunky black rubber buttons, a 25-foot Stanley tape measure pulled out 18 inches, a folded paper kitchen floor-plan blueprint with hand-drawn pencil dimension marks (no readable numbers, just abstract pencil marks and arrows), a stub of carpenter pencil, a Milwaukee impact driver in red and black at the edge of the frame. Plywood grain visible through scattered sawdust. Shot with a 50mm normal lens straight down, slightly cool overhead workshop fluorescent lighting, even shadows. Slightly desaturated except for the brand-red Milwaukee impact body. Documentary, working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright. No readable text, no logos.
